operating_state: Total revenue increased to $1.1 billion driven by Liberty Transaction acquisition of 519 properties contributing $80 million incremental NOI. Same-store NOI grew 4.2% on net effective basis with positive rent change continuing across all quarters since 2013. Consolidated occupancy remained strong at 95.9% with 13.6% estimated spread between in-place rents and current market rates. Core FFO increased to $742 million from $599 million while net earnings decreased to $366 million.
cost_structure: Real Estate Operations NOI expanded to $747 million from $639 million primarily from Liberty acquisition scale benefits and organic rent growth. G&A expenses rose due to higher compensation costs linked to share price appreciation, though G&A as percentage of assets under management continued declining through scale efficiencies. Real estate depreciation increased reflecting portfolio expansion.
material_events: Completed $2.0 billion senior note issuances including euro-denominated tranches across 2032 and 2041 maturities. Redeemed $1.5 billion higher-rate debt extending weighted average maturity to 11 years and reducing effective interest rate to 1.7%. Generated $793 million net proceeds from property contributions to co-investment ventures and dispositions realizing $190 million net gains. Quarterly common stock dividend increased to $0.63 per share from $0.58.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty across global markets though minimal direct business impact observed. Foreign currency exposure managed through co-investment venture structures and functional currency borrowings providing natural hedges. E-commerce acceleration and supply chain resilience trends driving structural demand increases for logistics real estate.
forward_outlook: Development activity accelerating with $3.7 billion TEI consolidated pipeline 61.8% leased. Management expects continued rent growth momentum from 13.6% in-place to market rent spread over multiple years. Available liquidity of $4.9 billion plus additional credit facility supports growth capital deployment. Recent debt issuance proceeds will redeem higher-rate notes due 2024 and 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Debt covenant compliance maintained with A3/A- credit ratings. Outstanding equity commitments to co-investment ventures through 2028 with partner commitments providing expansion capacity. Unconsolidated co-investment ventures carried third-party debt with 27.9% weighted average loan-to-value ratio though the company provided no debt guarantees.
